# André Bowen — Full Content

> Australian oil painter working in still life, landscape, and the figure. Based in the Northern Rivers of New South Wales.

Website: https://andrebowen.com
Contact: andrebowen@me.com

---

## Biography

André Bowen (born 1974, Australia) is an oil painter whose work spans still life, landscape, and the figure. After studying at the National Art School in Sydney and the Art Students League of New York, he spent a decade living and painting in Leipzig and Berlin, Germany, before returning to Australia.

His landscapes are painted on location — in the Northern Rivers of New South Wales, on the New Zealand South Island during a residency at the Nock Art Foundation in Queenstown, and across the streets and canals of Berlin. His still lifes share the same directness: objects observed closely, painted from life in natural light.

André was awarded the Defiance Gallery Prize at the Paddington Art Prize in 2018, which led to a solo exhibition at Defiance Gallery, Mary Place, in 2019. His work is held in private collections in Australia, the United States, and Europe.

He is currently living and working in the Northern Rivers of New South Wales.

---

## Still Life

URL: https://andrebowen.com/still-life.html

- Abyssinian Roses, 2016. Oil on linen panel. 35 × 28 cm.
- Flowers in a Striped Vase, 2019. Oil on panel. 41 × 50 cm.

- Madrid Silver, 2017. Oil on panel. 40 × 30 cm.
- Flowers in a Mason Jar, 2016. Oil on linen panel. 35 × 28 cm.
- Jar for Joko (Turps), 2016. Oil on panel. 40 × 30 cm.
- Jar for Mike (Oil), 2016. Oil on panel. 40 × 30 cm.
- Outdoor Still Life, 2016. Oil on linen panel. 40 × 30 cm.
- Leipzig Studio, 2009. Oil on canvas. 70 × 50 cm.
- Leipzig Studio (Blue Chair), 2009. Oil on canvas. 35 × 28 cm.
- Flowers, 2009. Oil on canvas. 35 × 25 cm.
- Chair (Shuttle), 2005. Tempera on linen panel. 60 × 75 cm.
- Studio Corner IV, 2025. Oil on canvas. 70 × 50 cm.
- Studio Window, 2025. Oil on panel. 50 × 70 cm.
- Studio Table Leipzig, 2010. Oil on canvas. 50 × 75 cm.
- Studio Table (Unfinished), 2019. Oil on canvas. 70 × 90 cm.

## Curriculum Vitae

### Studies

- 2006: BFA Honours, Painting — UNSW College of Fine Arts, Sydney
- 2003–05: BFA Painting — National Art School, Sydney
- 1998–99: The Art Students League of New York, New York

### Solo Exhibitions

- 2019: Defiance Gallery at Mary Place, Paddington, Sydney
- 2010: Defiance Gallery, Paddington, Sydney

### Group Exhibitions

- 2024: Defiance Gallery at Mary Place, Paddington, Sydney
- 2018: Paddington Art Prize — winner, Defiance Gallery Award, Sydney
- 2018: Hurford Hardwood Portrait Prize — Lismore Regional Gallery, Finalist
- 2018: Border Art Prize — Tweed Regional Gallery, Finalist
- 2018: BAM Art Prize, Byron Bay
- 2018: Gallery 7, Byron Bay
- 2013: Doug Moran National Portrait Prize
- 2013: Fishers Ghost Art Prize, Campbelltown
- 2013: Manning Art Prize — Manning Regional Gallery, Runner-up
- 2011: Defiance Gallery, Sydney
- 2010: A Perfect Day to Chase Tornadoes, Bethanien House, Berlin
- 2008: Defiance Gallery, Sydney
- 2008: Artbreak Gallery, Brooklyn, New York
- 2007: Same Place, Many Views: Painting the Australian Landscape — Defiance Gallery, Sydney
- 2006: Kudos Gallery, Paddington, Sydney
- 2005: FONAS Show — Cell Block Theatre, National Art School, Sydney
- 2005: MCQ International Art Prize — Chifley Plaza, Sydney
- 1998: Chinese Theatre — Carlton Arms Hotel, New York

### Awards

- 2018: Paddington Art Prize — Defiance Prize
- 2013: Manning Art Prize — Runner-up, Manning Regional Gallery
- 2005: Parker's Art Prize — National Art School, Sydney
- 2005: William Fletcher Trust Drawing Prize, Sydney

### Residencies

- 2019: Nock Art Foundation, Queenstown, New Zealand

### Collections

- ICAA, Sydney
- The Art Cannot Be Damaged, New York
- The Molongo Collection, Canberra

---

## Bibliography

- 2023: *Nathan* — Marie Eve Nadeau (front cover artwork)
- 2018: *Bangalow Herald*
- 2016: *Black Night, Black Knight* — Mike Tyler (front cover artwork)
- 2006: *New Blood* — Australian Art Review, March 2006
- 2002: *New York Hotel Story* — Nathalie Daoust
